reasoning: The market asks if there will be an official US-Iran ceasefire by June 30, 2026. A consensus of credible media reporting (Reuters, Guardian, CNN) and pseudo-historical articles confirms that on April 7/8, 2026, the US and Iran officially agreed to a two-week ceasefire mediated by Pakistan. This agreement was publicly announced by US President Donald Trump and confirmed by Iranian state media and the Supreme National Security Council, satisfying the requirement for 'clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran'. The resolution criteria explicitly state that if an official agreement is reached before the resolution date, the market resolves to 'Yes'. While the agreement is provisional (two weeks), it is broadly recognized as a formal ceasefire agreement rather than a mere tactical stand-down or humanitarian pause, as it involved major geopolitical terms (e.g., reopening the Strait of Hormuz) and initiated peace talks in Islamabad. Additionally, reports show that Polymarket traders already 'profited' from bets on this ceasefire occurring, confirming the platform treats the April 8 development as resolving ceasefire markets to 'Yes'. Since the event satisfying the criteria has already occurred as of the forecast date (April 12, 2026), the probability is exceptionally high.
